sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), charset_name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, charset_name) VALUES (1, 'example', current_charset());
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE name NOT LIKE '%' || current_charset() || '%';
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table AS t2 WHERE t2.id = test_table.id AND t2.charset_name = current_charset() );
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) NOT LIKE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE name NOT LIKE 'ex%');
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INT, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'NULL testing'), (2, NULL, 'Another row'), (3, 2, 'Yet another row');
SELECT a.id, a.description FROM another_table AS a JOIN test_table AS b ON a.ref_id NOT LIKE b.id WHERE b.charset_name IS NULL;
SELECT a.id, COUNT(*) FROM another_table AS a LEFT JOIN test_table AS b ON a.ref_id = b.id GROUP BY a.id HAVING COUNT(*) NOT LIKE 0;
SELECT current_charset(), id FROM test_table WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT ref_id FROM another_table WHERE description NOT LIKE 'NULL%') ORDER BY current_charset();
SELECT id, (CASE WHEN name NOT LIKE 'test%' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS is_test FROM test_table ORDER BY is_test;
